column1: # Per-repo data for scripts/post_notices.sh. Tab-separated, five columns:
# - skip_reason: if non-empty, this row is skipped with the reason printed
# - extra_opening: optional sentence prepended to the standard body (used
#   for repos where the repo name doesn't match the catalogue entry name)
# Skip rows (own project, soft-touch projects that need a personal email
# instead) are pre-populated. Edit if your judgement differs.
# Order is by camp (syntactic → verification → orchestration → unclassified)
# matching the catalogue's organisation. The script processes top-to-bottom
# so editing this order changes which repos get posted to first.
# Comment lines (#) and blank lines are skipped.

column5: The catalogue entry covers B-IR — one of the three exploration languages described in your blog post (alongside TBIR and Loom). Let me know if I have conflated something.

column4: thought experiment / art piece — handle personally

column5: The catalogue entry is for Raskell — the repo here is `hx`. Let me know if I have conflated something.

column4: tongue-in-cheek pre-alpha, anonymous author — handle personally
